rag_rules: Place AGREE annexes in component type scopes for interface assumptions/guarantees and implementation scopes for bindings.
Use only visible identifiers from the current component scope in AGREE annexes.
generic_rules: Component type contracts should reference only visible ports and features at that scope.
Implementation contracts should use assign for output bindings and avoid guarantee statements.
Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers not present in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Implementation-level bindings must be placed in implementation scopes after subcomponents and connections.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not mix component type and implementation scope elements in the same annex.
